instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-divides` that signals an error if a divisor does not evenly divide a value; otherwise returns the value. Each argument is evaluated exactly once.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 3) (#:G2 AMOUNT))
            (UNLESS (ZEROP (MOD #:G2 #:G1))
              (ERROR "~S does not divide ~S" #:G1 #:G2))
            #:G2)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 3) (#:G113 AMOUNT))
            (UNLESS (ZEROP (MOD #:G113 #:G112))
              (ERROR "~S does not divide ~S" #:G112 #:G113))
            #:G113)

--- stderr ---
